body.title: Vetinari: directive lifecycle tracking via cc_queue polling
body.result: check_directive_progress() + get_directive_status() + _query_ticket_status(); shim 5-min poll loop; 9 tests covering all-open/partial/all-closed/missing/snapshot
body.intention: I intend that Vetinari polls cc_queue for child ticket status and updates directive flat-file state so the directive lifecycle is always queryable.
body.description:
Vetinari's receive_health_rollup() tracks factory health, not ticket completion. There is no connection between a directive's child tickets and whether the directive is 'done'.

Add check_directive_progress(directive_id) to VetinariDevice: load child_ticket_ids from directive state, call cc_queue.py show for each, aggregate into {open: N, in_progress: N, closed: N}, update directive state with progress snapshot. Add get_directive_status(directive_id) -> str: 'active'|'completed'|'blocked'|'awaiting_clarification'. Add a periodic poll hook (call from VetinariShim's idle loop every 5 min).

**Affected files:** devices/vetinari/device.py (add check_directive_progress(), get_directive_status()), devices/vetinari/shim.py (add poll loop), tests/test_vetinari_progress.py (NEW)
**Design rules:** cc_queue poll via subprocess (same as other integrations); directive state flat-file JSON; 'completed' only when all child tickets are closed
**Scope boundary:** IN — progress poll, directive state update, get_directive_status(); OUT — deployment signal (separate ticket), UI/reporting
**Completion criteria:** check_directive_progress() with all-closed children → status='completed'; with some open → status='active'; directive state file updated with progress snapshot; 5+ tests covering: all-open, partial-closed, all-closed, missing ticket (graceful)
